metadata.title: Map a Hard Week to the Hazard-Carter Adjustment Framework
metadata.note: CLI video in the REQUIRED spine: B00 intro · B01 PROBLEM · B02 ASK · B03 CODE · B04 OUTPUT(slate) · B05 CHANGE · B06 slate · B07 SUMMARY · B08 NEXT STEPS · B09 outro.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: B00: palette=claude but the cold open is 'NikBearBrownOpen' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B09: palette=claude but the outro is 'NikBearBrownOutro' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI, then take it apart.

beats.role_note: the problem, the context, the stakes — BEFORE the CLI
beats.narration_text: A hard week in college is never just one thing. It is a chemistry exam, a roommate conflict, a financial aid email that doesn't make sense, a lecture you couldn't follow, homesickness hitting at midnight, and a new friend group forming without you — all in the same seven days. When it all hits at once, students often treat it as one undifferentiated crisis. The Hazard-Carter framework gives each event its own domain — and its own door.
beats.visual_intent: title card: six events listed as a stack; the question: 'Six events — or one crisis?' — no terminal yet

build.suggested: suggested prompt: A hard week in college is never just one thing. It is a chemistry exam, a roommate conflict, a financial aid email that doesn't make sense, a lecture you couldn

beats.narration_text: One command. Give Claude the six domains from Hazard and Carter 2018 — Academic, Cultural, Emotional, Financial, Intellectual, Social — and map six real hard-week events to those domains, each with one campus resource you can walk through.

props.command: claude "Using the Hazard and Carter (2018) framework of 6 college adjustment domains — Academic, Cultural, Emotional, Financial, Intellectual, Social — map this hard week to its domains and identify one campus resource for each: [student failed chemistry exam, roommate conflict, financial aid email, confusing lecture, homesick, new friend group forming without them]. Format as a 6-row table: Domain | What qualifies | This week's event | Campus door."

beats.narration_text: The output is a markdown file — adjustment domains dot md. Six rows, one per domain. Each row maps the abstract domain definition to the specific event that landed this week, and names one campus door: tutoring center, residential life, counseling services, financial aid office, academic advising, student activities.

props.code:
# adjustment_domains.md — domain mapping table output

| Domain       | What qualifies              | This week's event               | Campus door              |
|--------------|-----------------------------|---------------------------------|--------------------------|
| Academic     | Coursework, grades, skills  | Failed chemistry exam           | Tutoring Center / TA hrs |
| Cultural     | Identity, campus belonging  | New friend group excluding you  | Multicultural Center     |
| Emotional    | Mood, coping, mental health | Homesick at midnight            | Counseling Services      |
| Financial    | Aid, budget, unexpected cost| Confusing financial aid email   | Financial Aid Office     |
| Intellectual | Engagement, curiosity       | Confusing lecture               | Academic Advising        |
| Social       | Relationships, community    | Roommate conflict               | Residential Life / RA    |

beats.narration_text: The table lands. Six rows, six doors. The hard week did not get smaller — but it got named. Each event belongs to exactly one domain, and each domain has a campus resource designed for exactly that kind of problem.
beats.visual_intent: screen recording of the six-row mapping table in terminal — each row complete, Campus door column visible

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The table lands. Six rows, six doors. The hard week did not get smaller — but it got named. Each event belongs to exactly one domain, and each domain has a camp

beats.narration_text: Now triage it. Ask Claude to add a Priority column — which domain needs to be addressed in the next 48 hours versus the next two weeks — and a one-sentence action for each high-priority item.

props.command: claude "Add a Priority column (48hr / 2-week) to the Hazard-Carter table and a one-sentence Next Action for each HIGH priority domain — based on which adjustment failures most directly threaten academic standing in the next week."

beats.narration_text: The revised table adds the triage column. Academic and Financial land in the 48-hour bucket — the exam failure and the financial aid confusion both have time-sensitive consequences. The others are real, but they have a two-week window. That separation is the first decision the framework gives you.
beats.visual_intent: screen recording of updated table with Priority and Next Action columns — Academic and Financial marked 48hr, specific actions visible

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The revised table adds the triage column. Academic and Financial land in the 48-hour bucket — the exam failure and the financial aid confusion both have time-se

beats.narration_text: Hazard and Carter's six domains turn a hard week from an undifferentiated wall into six addressable problems. The framework does not fix anything — but it tells you which door to walk through first, and which campus resource is already paid for and waiting on the other side.
beats.visual_intent: recap card: 'Six events → six domains → six doors'; triage result: Academic + Financial = 48hr priority

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Hazard and Carter's six domains turn a hard week from an undifferentiated wall into six addressable problems. The framework does not fix anything — but it tells

beats.narration_text: Your move: the next time a week feels like everything is wrong at once, paste your specific events into Claude with this prompt. Name the domain for each one. Walk through one door this week — the highest-priority one. The goal is not to solve everything; it is to not let the undifferentiated weight of it cause you to do nothing.
beats.visual_intent: next-steps card: 1) paste your own hard-week events, 2) identify domain for each, 3) walk through one door in 48 hours

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Your move: the next time a week feels like everything is wrong at once, paste your specific events into Claude with this prompt. Name the domain for each one. W

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI. Then take it apart. At Nik Bear Brown, nikbearbrown dot com.
